summary: Heavily obfuscated index.js with no traced behavior
details: The package's index.js is heavily obfuscated, exhibiting classic string-array rotation patterns (while-loop with repeated shift() calls and large hex offsets). Obfuscation in an npm package's main entry is suspicious because it deliberately impedes review of what the code does on require, but the specific runtime behavior — whether it performs network I/O, reads credentials, or executes shell commands — was not confirmed in this analysis. The package name suggests a SQLite helper, which would not normally require obfuscation. Human review is warranted to deobfuscate index.js and determine whether the runtime behavior is benign or constitutes credential theft, exfiltration, or remote code execution.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
